title: The Salt Wife
author: Barış Kısır
description: A folklorist on a remote Hebridean island discovers that the local tradition of salt wives — women who marry the sea — is not just a legend.
long_description: Dr. Elara Morrow arrives on the remote Hebridean island of Stiorra to document its oral traditions. She learns of the salt wives — women who, according to local legend, marry the sea itself. What she dismisses as metaphor proves to be a living, repeating tradition. As midsummer approaches and the island prepares for its next bride, Morrow finds herself drawn into something far older and darker than any folklore she has ever studied. Literary folk horror in the tradition of Machen and Hodgson.

characters.name: Dr. Elara Morrow
characters.description: A 34-year-old folklorist from the University of Glasgow who comes to Stiorra to document oral traditions and becomes the witness to the salt wife ritual.

characters.name: Morag MacLeod
characters.description: A 78-year-old island elder who was once chosen as a salt wife but refused, bearing the scars and the burden of her refusal through three generations.

characters.name: The Sea Husband
characters.description: The entity beneath the waves that courts and claims a bride every sixty years, speaking through dreams and leaving gifts of shell and kelp.

characters.name: Catriona MacLeod
characters.description: A fifteen-year-old girl chosen as the next salt wife, Morag's granddaughter, who accepts her fate with a mixture of fear and peace.

characters.name: Iain MacKenzie
characters.description: The ferryman, a weathered man who knows when to stay silent and understands the sea's demands.

characters.name: Father Thomas
characters.description: The island priest who tries to reconcile his Christian faith with older truths, keeping a tide clock on his church wall.

short_description: A folklorist on a remote Hebridean island discovers that the legend of women marrying the sea is still alive.
